says Pakistan primary mediator in Iran negotiations | ARY News \n\nCDF Munir heads to Iran as Islamabad pushes Tehran-Washington mediation: security sources\n\nPakistan 'primary' mediator in Iran negotiations: Rubio \nUS Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Friday reiterated that Pakistan remained the \"primary\" negotiator in Washington’s negotiations with Iran to end the war.\nSpeaking to the media in Sweden's Helsingborg, he expressed hope that Washington would secure a deal with Tehran.\n\nQatari negotiating team in Tehran to help secure US-Iran deal to end war: source \nA Qatari negotiating team arrived in Tehran on Friday in coordination with United States to help secure a deal to end the war with Iran and resolve outstanding issues, a source with knowledge of the matter told Reuters on Friday.\nDoha, which has worked as a mediator in the Gaza war and other areas international tensions, had till now distanced itself from playing a mediation role in the Iran war after it came under attack from Iranian missiles and drones during the latest conflict.\n\nField Marshal Asim Munir leaves for Iran: security sources \nChief of Defence Force (CDF) and Chief of Army Staff (COAS) Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir has left for an official visit to Iran, said security sources, adding that he will meet with key Iranian dignitaries there.\nCDF Munir will discuss Iran-US talks
